explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: A patent describes a method for detecting driver traffic distress using machine learning. This patent mentions the generation of a "bias score" for driver thermal change estimates.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: This patent describes a method for detecting driver traffic distress using machine learning, providing evidence for **fairness** and **transparency**. The patent explicitly mentions the generation of a "bias score" for driver thermal change estimates, directly addressing fairness. It also details various machine learning algorithms and models (e.g., GMM, FCN, RNN, LSTM-GRU, CNN) and training methods (backpropagation, expectation maximization), demonstrating transparency in the AI system's design and capabilities.
relevant_sources.title: Traffic distress and road rage detection methods

governance.findings: Sanmina's annual report indicates a strategic intent to invest in artificial intelligence, factory automation, and robotics to improve efficiency, and mentions the development of next-generation semiconductor equipment for AI solutions. A case study details the implementation of an AI-powered Zero Trust Exchange platform, highlighting the use of AI-powered threat intelligence to mitigate risk and listing AI-related security functions. Additionally, a patent describes a method for detecting driver traffic distress using machine learning.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The Sanmina Corporation Form 10-K (2024) provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. This annual report indicates Sanmina's strategic intent to invest in artificial intelligence, factory automation, and robotics to improve efficiency, demonstrating a commitment to AI as a core business strategy. The mention of developing next-generation semiconductor equipment for AI solutions further supports this governance pillar by highlighting their forward-looking approach to AI integration.
relevant_sources.title: Sanmina Corporation Form 10-K (2024)

relevant_sources.summary: This case study provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. It details Sanmina's implementation of an AI-powered Zero Trust Exchange platform, highlighting the use of AI-powered threat intelligence to mitigate risk. The document implies a commitment to AI governance and security practices by listing AI-related security functions and describing the utilization of AI for threat mitigation.
relevant_sources.title: Sanmina Revs Up Security, Profitability & M&A Strategies with Zero Trust for Users and Workloads

The patent explicitly mentions the generation of a "bias score" for driver thermal change estimates, directly addressing fairness. It also details various machine learning algorithms and models (e.g., GMM, FCN, RNN, LSTM-GRU, CNN) and training methods (backpropagation, expectation maximization), demonstrating transparency in the AI system's design and capabilities.

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: A patent details various machine learning algorithms and models, including GMM, FCN, RNN, LSTM-GRU, and CNN, used in a system for detecting driver traffic distress. The patent also describes training methods such as backpropagation and expectation maximization for these models.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This patent describes a method for detecting driver traffic distress using machine learning, providing evidence for **fairness** and **transparency**. The patent explicitly mentions the generation of a "bias score" for driver thermal change estimates, directly addressing fairness. It also details various machine learning algorithms and models (e.g., GMM, FCN, RNN, LSTM-GRU, CNN) and training methods (backpropagation, expectation maximization), demonstrating transparency in the AI system's design and capabilities.
relevant_sources.title: Traffic distress and road rage detection methods

summary.overall_findings: Sanmina's published materials detail various machine learning algorithms and models, such as GMM, FCN, RNN, LSTM-GRU, and CNN, used in a system for detecting driver traffic distress, addressing the transparency pillar. These disclosures, along with a patent mentioning the generation of a "bias score" for driver thermal change estimates, contribute to documented evidence across 3 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Governance is also addressed through an annual report indicating a strategic intent to invest in artificial intelligence, factory automation, and robotics, with transparency, fairness, and governance all having documented policies. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability, oversight, and 2 additional pillars, based on a review of 10 publicly available sources.
